title: scaevola browniana
intro: Scaevola browniana, also known as Australian fan flower or fan flower, is a flowering plant native to Australia, characterized by its delicate, fan-shaped purple, pink, or white flowers. Traditionally, Scaevola browniana has been used in herbal medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, including soothing skin irritations, reducing fever, and aiding in digestion. Scaevola browniana can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, making it a versatile addition to herbal remedies.
